title: Hedge
author: Jane Delury
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Hedge" book by Jane Delury?
Options:
A. Peter's personality was nothing like Leonard's. When Maud got to Peter's house, he came down the steps to meet her as she was walking up. He said he hoped she would get there ok and rushed to open his car door. Inside, Ella was sitting in bed eating a popsicle. Maud checked her forehead for fever but it didn't seem very hot. Ella complained her throat was burning and her dad was angry. Maud ignored the comment and adjusted the pillows. Ella said she assumed she would be stuck in the nurse's office all day. Maud pointed out it had only been an hour. She said it had worked out fine, right? As Maud went back down the hall, she was irritated with Peter and Ella for acting like she had done something bad by being preoccupied with work, as Peter frequently was.
B. Peter's personality was nothing similar to Leonard's. When she got to Peter's house, he came down the steps to greet her as she was walking up. He said he hoped she would make it there ok and rushed to open his car door. Inside, Ella was in bed with a popsicle. Maud checked her forehead for fever but it didn't feel very hot. Ella complained her throat was on fire and her dad was upset. Maud ignored the comment and fluffed the pillows. Ella said she thought she would have to sit in the nurse's office all day. Maud pointed out it had only been an hour. She said it had turned out fine, right? Going back down the hall, she was annoyed with Peter and Ella for acting like she had done something wrong by being preoccupied with work, as Peter often was.
C. Peter and Leonard were very different. When she got to Peter's house, he came down the steps to greet her as she was coming up. He said he hoped she would make it there ok and hurried to his car. Inside, Ella was in bed eating a popsicle. Maud checked her forehead for fever but it didn't feel very warm. Ella complained her throat was burning and that her dad was angry. Maud ignored the comment and fluffed the pillows. Ella said she thought she would have to stay in the nurse's office all day. Maud pointed out it was only an hour. She said it had turned out fine, hadn't it? As Maud went back down the hall, she was annoyed with Peter and Ella for acting like she had done something wrong by being caught up with work, like Peter always was.
D. And Peter was nothing like Leonard. When she arrived at the house, he came down the steps as she came up. Hoping Ill make it, he said and yanked open his car door. Inside, Ella sat in bed with a popsicle. Maud put her hand on her forehead, which didnt feel too hot. My throats on fire and Dads pissed, Ella said. Ignoring the comment, Maud plumped the pillows. I thought I was going to sit in the nurses office all day, Ella said. It was only an hour, Maud said. It turned out okay, didnt it? Going back down the hall, she was annoyed with Peter, with both of them, really, for acting as if shed done something wrong by being caught up in her work, as Peter always was.
Answer:

He said he hoped she would get there ok and rushed to open his car door. Inside, Ella was sitting in bed eating a popsicle. Maud checked her forehead for fever but it didn't seem very hot. Ella complained her throat was burning and her dad was angry. Maud ignored the comment and adjusted the pillows. Ella said she assumed she would be stuck in the nurse's office all day. Maud pointed out it had only been an hour. She said it had worked out fine, right? As Maud went back down the hall, she was irritated with Peter and Ella for acting like she had done something bad by being preoccupied with work, as Peter frequently was.
